brief_justification: Correctly computes 8% (0.8s ÷ 0.1s × 1%) and connects it to the 20% observed lift with valid causal framing; goes three levels deep: arithmetic → confound identification → SUTVA violation → actionable decomposition recommendations; explicitly names suppressed signals at the top and applies them throughout the response; rechecks arithmetic and cross-validates against total lift (8/20 = 40%) but does not attempt re-derivation from an independent angle; hedges with 'potentially attributable' and '~12%' but does not formally state confidence levels or enumerate what would change the conclusion; mentions two remediation paths (re-run with normalized CDN or decompose remaining lift) but does not evaluate their tradeoffs or probability of changing the outcome; every step is traceable — setup, calculation, SUTVA check, and contamination flag are all shown with explicit derivations; directly avoids the correlation-only attribution anti-pattern and explicitly names it as the error the product team is making; zero evidence of any suppressed failure modes and explicitly calls out three of them by name in the opening block; follows the topology closely — fork into factual/counterfactual, join to compute causal impact, SUTVA gate flagged as contaminated, concludes with invalid attribution warning matching the OUT:invalid_attribution path.
